items.headline: Live Updates: Pistons-Cavaliers, Thunder-Lakers clash in pivotal Game 3s
items.description: Check out the best from Saturday's action in the 2026 NBA Playoffs with our live blog, featuring Pistons-Cavs and Thunder-Lakers.

items.headline: Starting 5: Wemby invades Ant’s house, Knicks go up 3-0, top seeds tip off Game 3s today
items.description: Wemby and Edwards’ rare duel, Brunson & NY storm Philly, Jared McCain aides OKC, Pistons’ Bickerstaff crafts comeback culture.

items.headline: Denver’s playoff struggle didn’t cost David Adelman. The roster, though, could be wide open
items.description: The president of the Denver Nuggets says his faith in coach David Adelman remains strong despite the team's early playoff exit.

items.headline: Joel Embiid returns from sore ankle and hip, but he’s not enough for Sixers as Knicks go up 3-0
items.description: Joel Embiid is one loss away from an early offseason, despite returning from injuries to try and make the East semis competitive.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Thunder-Lakers Game 3
items.description: The Lakers could benefit from a return to basics, especially if Thunder win the minutes Shai Gilgeous-Alexander sits in Game 3.

items.headline: OG Anunoby sidelined for Game 3 against 76ers with hamstring strain
items.description: Knicks forward listed as day-to-day with right hamstring strain suffered in Game 2 of Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Pistons-Cavaliers Game 3
items.description: A potential James Harden bounce back and Tobias Harris' scoring streak could prove to be key in Game 3 between Pistons and Cavs.

items.headline: Recap: Knicks go up 3-0, Spurs take 2-1 lead behind Wemby's historic night
items.description: Check out the best from Friday's action in the 2026 NBA Conference Semifinals with our live blog, featuring Knicks-76ers, Spurs-Wolves.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Wemby masterclass leads Spurs to Game 3 win
items.description: Victor Wembanyama outduels Anthony Edwards to lead Spurs to 2-1 series advantage heading into Game 4.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Knicks turn back repeated rallies in Philadelphia, stake 3-0 series lead over 76ers
items.description: Jalen Brunson's 33 points help the Knicks beat the 76ers in Game 3, leaving them one win from a return trip to the East Finals.

items.headline: Dallas Mavericks name Mike Schmitz general manager
items.description: Schmitz will help lead the Mavs' scouting, player personnel, strategic planning and organizational collaboration across departments.

items.headline: Raptors' Brandon Ingram undergoes surgery to address ongoing right heel pain
items.description: The Raptors forward is expected to fully recover and be ready for training camp in September.

items.headline: The Athletic: Meet Karl-Anthony Towns’ biggest fan — the mother of a Marine killed in Afghanistan
items.description: In 2012, Towns, already a star at 16, paid tribute to a fallen Marine who had attended his school. The gesture sparked a lasting friendship.

items.headline: 2026 NBA Draft Lottery: Each team's representatives for Sunday's Draft Lottery
items.description: From franchise icons to current players and team executives, here's who will represent each of the 14 NBA Draft Lottery teams.

items.headline: FIBA Hall of Famer Clarisse Machanguana rebuilding after catastrophic flooding in Mozambique
items.description: Former WNBA player Clarisse Machanguana and her foundation are helping rebuild Mozambique after the Incomati River flooded the nation.
